topics.name: 100–200 Level: Pre-clinical Sciences — Anatomy, Physiology, and Biochemistry (UNTH)
topics.description: ANA 101 Gross Anatomy, PHS 211 Physiology, BCH 211 Biochemistry. Based at UNTH Enugu.

subtopics.name: ANA 101 & ANA 211: Gross and Microscopic Anatomy at UNN
objectives: Identify and describe the structures of all major body systems in cadaveric dissection at UNN College of Medicine
Interpret histology slides of major organs and tissues in practical classes

subtopics.name: PHS 211 & BCH 211: Physiology and Medical Biochemistry at UNN
objectives: Explain cardiovascular, respiratory, renal, and endocrine physiological mechanisms and their clinical relevance
Describe major metabolic pathways and the biochemical basis of diseases such as diabetes and sickle cell anaemia

topics.name: 300 Level: Pathology, Microbiology, Pharmacology, and Community Medicine
topics.description: PATH 301 Pathology, MIC 311 Medical Microbiology, PHAR 321 Pharmacology, COM 331 Community Medicine.

subtopics.name: PATH 301 & MIC 311: Pathology and Microbiology
objectives: Describe mechanisms of cellular injury, acute and chronic inflammation, and neoplastic transformation
Identify common Nigerian pathogens — Plasmodium, Mycobacterium tuberculosis, Salmonella typhi — and their management

subtopics.name: COM 331: Community Medicine and Epidemiology
objectives: Apply epidemiological methods to investigate disease outbreaks in Nigerian communities
Describe primary health care (PHC) strategy, immunisation programmes, and Alma-Ata Declaration principles

topics.name: 400–600 Level: Clinical Rotations — Medicine, Surgery, Paediatrics, O&G (UNTH)
topics.description: CL 401 Internal Medicine, CL 411 Surgery, CL 421 Paediatrics, CL 431 O&G, CL 599 Final MBBS at UNTH.

subtopics.name: CL 401 & CL 411: Medicine and Surgery at UNTH Enugu
objectives: Diagnose and manage common medical and surgical conditions in Southeast Nigerian patients at UNTH
Perform clinical procedures including venepuncture, IV cannulation, catheterisation, and wound suturing

subtopics.name: CL 599: Final MBBS Professional Examinations at UNN
objectives: Demonstrate clinical competence in written OSCE and oral examinations in all clinical disciplines
Obtain provisional registration with the Medical and Dental Council of Nigeria (MDCN)
